onlyInPassB.key: authority|fdse.microservice.config.SecurityConfig|authority|requiresRole|ADMIN role required on POST, PUT, DELETE /api/v1/stationservice/stations
capability|security.service.SecurityServiceImpl|capability|servesQuery|check account order count in last hour and total valid orders against configured thresholds
contract|fdse.microservice.entity.Station|specification|ensures|normalized station name unique, enforced by column constraint and by service-level findByName check before create
contract|food.entity.StationFoodStore|specification|ensures|non-null station_name and unique (station_name, store_name) pair enforced by table index
effect|food.init.InitData|effect|haltsProcess|exits process with status 1 when the foodstores resource file has an unexpected line format during startup seeding
semantic|fdse.microservice.entity.Station|semantic|identifiedBy|looked up by generated UUID id, or by normalized unique name including a native SQL IN-clause batch lookup

semantic|fdse.microservice.entity.Station|semantic|identifiedBy|station name identity is normalized by stripping spaces and lowercasing with Locale.ROOT before it is stored or compared, in every constructor and setter path

semantic|food.controller.StationFoodController|semantic|meansInUse|the route template segment for this handler is named {stationId}, but the bound handler parameter is named stationName with no explicit @PathVariable value, so the path segment is bound positionally rather than by matching name
semantic|food.entity.StationFoodStore|semantic|identifiedBy|looked up by generated UUID store_id, or by station_name which is only guaranteed unique in combination with store_name

semantic|security.service.SecurityServiceImpl|semantic|meansInUse|application.yml declares order-service.url and order-other-service.url properties, but the service URL actually used at the call site is built in code from a hardcoded service name with an http prefix rather than injected via @Value from those properties

semantic|security.service.SecurityServiceImpl|semantic|meansInUse|named security config entries max_order_1_hour and max_order_not_use are read and parsed as integer thresholds that gate whether an account may keep placing orders
state|food.entity.StationFoodStore|state|persistsIn|station_food_store table with unique index station_store_idx on (station_name, store_name)
